AI summary Have a model read this canvas for you.
Ask ChatGPT Ask Claude
Canvas 17 · Ops

External Services

Sardis is not a vertical monolith. It is a control plane above a carefully chosen set of third-party services — MPC custody, KYC, sanctions, card issuers, PSPs, ramps, RPC, rails, observability. This canvas enumerates every one, where it's wired, and why it beat its alternative.

Single source of truth Every config class below lives in packages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/config.py. Every env var uses a typed prefix (e.g. TURNKEY_, SARDIS_STRIGA_) so a missing value fails fast at boot, not at the first payment.
Group 01 · MPC / Signing

Private keys never live in Sardis.

Every wallet signature routes through an MPC provider. Turnkey is the default; Fireblocks, Circle's Programmable Wallets, and Lit Protocol are pluggable behind the same interface. Canvas 09 covers the wallet lifecycle.

ServiceConfig classEnv prefixStatusWhy
TurnkeyTurnkeyConfigTURNKEY_Live (primary)Sub-org per tenant, API-signed requests, deterministic HD addressing. Keys never leave Turnkey.
Fireblockspluggable—Interface-readyEnterprise alternative; picked on a per-merchant basis when compliance requires SOC2 Type II from the custody provider.
Circle Programmable Walletspluggable—Interface-readyFalls out naturally where Circle CPN / Gateway are already wired.
Lit Protocolpluggable—ResearchThreshold MPC with policy at the key — future path for "self-custodial agent" narratives.
Group 02 · KYC / AML

Compliance without a compliance team.

Canvas 12 has the full flow. Didit won the KYC slot on cost + velocity; Elliptic remains the AML/sanctions screen.

ServicePurposeEnv prefixStatusWhy
DiditKYC (identity verification)DIDIT_Live (primary)500 free/month, $0.33/check after; YC W26; clean v3 API with x-api-key auth; webhook working end-to-end. Replaced iDenfy (abandoned) and Persona (rejected).
EllipticAML + sanctions screeningELLIPTIC_LiveChain-aware sanctions screening; covers OFAC + sectoral lists; embedded in the Pre-Execution Pipeline as a fail-closed check.
Persona——RejectedPriced for enterprise; minimum commit too high for a single-founder startup.
iDenfy——AbandonedEarlier integration; replaced by Didit after onboarding friction.
Group 03 · Card issuing

Virtual cards + agent cards.

Multi-provider by design — CardStackConfig routes between Stripe, Lithic, Rain, Bridge, and Striga. One interface, five issuers. Stripe is the launch default; Lithic stays in sandbox until funding allows the $5M program commit.

ServiceConfig classEnv prefixStatusWhy
Stripe IssuingStripeConfigSTRIPE_Live (primary)Accessible without $5M program commit; pairs with existing Stripe Connect + Treasury plumbing. stablecoin_issuing_enabled flag flips on the USDC-settled variant.
LithicLithicConfigLITHIC_SandboxDeep controls (ASA webhooks, on-card policies). Held back until funding qualifies for the program.
RainRainConfigRAIN_Interface-readyStablecoin-native card program; drops in via CardStackConfig with zero core changes.
Bridge CardsBridgeCardsConfigBRIDGE_Interface-readyMulti-rail (ACH/SEPA/PIX/wire) — issuer + funding in one integration.
Striga CardsStrigaConfigSARDIS_STRIGA_Interface-readyEEA issuance with a clean SEPA/vIBAN story; complements Bridge outside the US.
Coinbase CDP (on-chain provider)CoinbaseConfigCOINBASE_LiveOn-chain provider slot — wallets as cards when an agent wants to transact purely on-chain.
Card stack routerCardStackConfigSARDIS_CARDS_LivePicks primary / fallback / on-chain provider, with per-org overrides via JSON.
Group 04 · PSPs & Payment Rails

Money movement beyond crypto.

ServiceConfig classEnv prefixStatusWhy
Stripe ConnectStripeConfigSTRIPE_LiveMerchant payouts (fiat off-ramp, US-first). connected_account_map_json maps tenants to Stripe accounts.
Stripe TreasuryStripeConfigSTRIPE_LiveFinancial-account-backed balance behind Issuing.
Stripe MPP (Tempo)StripeConfigSTRIPE_LiveEarly access since 2026-03-18 launch. Sardis is first-class on Tempo mainnet.
Stripe BillingStripeBillingConfigSTRIPE_BILLING_ReadySubscription billing path for the operator-facing product (post Atlas entity).
Lightspark (Grid API)LightsparkConfigSARDIS_LIGHTSPARK_SandboxLightning + UMA (uma_domain=sardis.sh) + FX + payouts. Gated by enabled flag.
Bridge (multi-rail)BridgeCardsConfigBRIDGE_Interface-readyOne integration for ACH + SEPA + PIX + wire; selects by default_rail + per-feature flags.
Striga (EEA banking)StrigaConfigSARDIS_STRIGA_SandboxvIBANs, SEPA, Lightning, standing orders, KYC — EEA-native fiat rails.
Group 05 · On/Off Ramps & Fiat Rails

USD ↔ USDC without writing a bank.

ServiceConfig classEnv prefixStatusWhy
Coinbase CDP OnrampCoinbaseConfigCOINBASE_LiveHosted fiat → USDC onramp, zero infra cost. topup_api_key drives the top-up variant.
Bridge OfframpBridgeCardsConfigBRIDGE_Interface-readyUSDC → fiat via ACH/SEPA/PIX.
Circle CPNCircleCPNConfigCIRCLE_CPN_LiveCircle Payments Network — funding / payouts; strategy=fiat_first + primary_adapter=circle_cpn by default in FundingRoutingConfig.
Circle GatewayCircleGatewayConfigCIRCLE_GATEWAY_Gatedx402 facilitator / nanopayments, enabled via x402_enabled.
Stripe (funding)StripeConfigSTRIPE_LiveStripe-backed funding for Issuing top-ups.
Funding routerFundingRoutingConfig—LiveStrategy: fiat_first / stablecoin_first / hybrid. Primary + fallback adapters are explicit.
Group 06 · Chain RPC

Broadcast + read.

RPC endpoints are env-var driven (SARDIS_BASE_RPC_URL, etc.) with public RPC as a fallback. Canvas 10 covers multi-chain settlement.

ServiceEnv varStatusWhy
Alchemy (Base)SARDIS_BASE_RPC_URLLive (primary)Fast, reliable, free-tier-generous; first choice for Base 8453.
Alchemy (Ethereum/Polygon/Arbitrum/Optimism)SARDIS_<chain>_RPC_URLLiveUniform provider across EVM chains.
Tempo RPCSARDIS_TEMPO_RPC_URL (default rpc.tempo.xyz)Live (mainnet 4217)First-party Tempo RPC; dedicated executor in sardis-chain/src/sardis_chain/tempo/executor.py.
Public RPCsfallback listFallback onlyUsed if primary RPC fails; never primary in prod.
Group 07 · Protocol Rails

Card network + credential standards.

ServiceConfig classEnv prefixStatusWhy
Visa TAPVisaTAPConfigVISA_TAP_SandboxToken Authentication Protocol — mTLS + TRID, signed tokens for agent identity at card-network level.
Mastercard Agent Pay / MDESMastercardConfigMASTERCARD_SandboxP12/PKCS12 certificate auth; MDES token provisioning for agent-initiated card transactions.
Delegated CredentialsDelegatedCredentialConfig—Interface-readyFramework for protocol-layer delegation (beyond AP2 / A2A).
x402X402ConfigX402_Gatedenabled / server_enabled / facilitator_enabled toggles; networks base,polygon,ethereum; optional Permit2.
ERC-8183ERC8183Config—LiveOn-chain agent-payment standard; 1% fee cap, USDC-only MVP; canvas 06.
Group 08 · Database & Cache

Stateful backbone.

ServiceEnv varStatusWhy
Neon PostgresDATABASE_URLLiveServerless Postgres; 107 migrations applied in order; branching for preview envs.
Upstash RedisSARDIS_REDIS_URLLiveRate-limiter (fail-closed if unreachable in prod), dedup store, replay cache, SSE fanout. Mandatory outside dev.
Group 09 · Observability

Knowing when something's wrong.

ServiceWhere it livesStatusWhy
PostHogapps/dashboard (posthog-js), VITE_POSTHOG_KEYLiveProduct analytics on the dashboard + landing. Funnels, retention, feature flags.
Sentry@sentry/nextjs (dashboard) + Python Sentry in APILiveErrors, performance, session replay. Integrated with Claude Code review via plugin.
Intercom@intercom/messenger-js-sdkLiveIn-app support chat for operators and merchants.
Uptime (internal)24 GitHub Actions workflows (uptime*.yml)LivePeriodic health checks of API, landing, dashboard; metrics published as graph artefacts.
Group 10 · Comms & Auth

Email + identity.

ServiceWhere it livesStatusWhy
ResendAPI notifications + dashboard transactional emailLiveTransactional email (receipts, approvals, security alerts). Simple API, modern DX.
better-authapps/dashboard (v1.5.6) + plugins (passkey, api-key, agent-auth, stripe, polar-sh)LiveEdDSA/JWKS sessions, passkeys, org roles, API-key issuance inside the dashboard.
Legacy FastAPI JWT (HS256)packages/sardis-apiLiveDual-path auth for SDK clients; bridged to better-auth via JWKS.
Stripe AtlasCompany entityLive (since 2026-03-21)Delaware C-corp "Sardis Labs, Inc." formed via Atlas; enables production Stripe + banking relationships.
Polar.shBilling (temp until Atlas Stripe clears)LiveBridge revenue path: Polar now, Stripe once Atlas entity is fully wired.
Turnstile + CaptchaFoxDashboard sign-up / resetLiveBot-prevention on public auth endpoints.
Group 11 · Trust Graph

FIDES — decentralized trust signalling.

ServiceConfig classEnv prefixStatusWhy
FIDES (Sardis protocol)FidesConfigSARDIS_FIDES_LiveAgent + merchant trust graph consulted in the Pre-Execution Pipeline. Fail-closed if unreachable.
Shape

How these services plug into the spine.

Every external dependency is behind an interface in sardis-core so individual providers can be swapped without touching business logic — the Didit-over-Persona swap happened with zero code churn outside the KYC adapter. 

flowchart LR
    API["Sardis API / Pre-Execution Pipeline"] --> KYC["Didit"]
    API --> AML["Elliptic"]
    API --> FID["FIDES trust graph"]
    API --> ORCH["PaymentOrchestrator"]

    ORCH --> MPC["Turnkey · Fireblocks · Circle · Lit"]
    ORCH --> CHAIN["Alchemy / Tempo / public RPC"]

    API --> FUND["Funding router"]
    FUND --> CCPN["Circle CPN"]
    FUND --> BRIDGE["Bridge (ACH/SEPA/PIX)"]
    FUND --> STRIPE["Stripe Treasury + Connect"]
    FUND --> ONRAMP["Coinbase CDP Onramp"]
    FUND --> STRIGA["Striga (EEA)"]
    FUND --> LS["Lightspark (Lightning / UMA)"]

    API --> CARDS["CardStack router"]
    CARDS --> STR["Stripe Issuing"]
    CARDS --> LIT["Lithic"]
    CARDS --> RAIN["Rain"]
    CARDS --> BRC["Bridge Cards"]
    CARDS --> STC["Striga Cards"]

    API --> NET["Visa TAP · Mastercard MDES · x402 · ERC-8183"]
    API --> DB["Neon Postgres"]
    API --> REDIS["Upstash Redis"]

    DASH["Dashboard · Next.js"] --> BA["better-auth (EdDSA/JWKS)"]
    DASH --> POLAR["Polar.sh billing"]
    DASH --> POSTHOG["PostHog"]
    DASH --> SENTRY["Sentry"]
    DASH --> INTERCOM["Intercom"]
Snapshot

Live vs. sandbox vs. interface-ready.

StatusServices
Live (production)Turnkey, Didit, Elliptic, Stripe (Issuing + Treasury + Connect + MPP), Coinbase CDP (Onramp), Circle CPN, Alchemy (Base, Ethereum, Polygon, Arbitrum, Optimism), Tempo RPC, Neon, Upstash, better-auth, Polar.sh, PostHog, Sentry, Intercom, Resend, Turnstile, FIDES, ERC-8183.
Sandbox / gatedLithic, Striga, Lightspark, Visa TAP, Mastercard MDES, x402, Circle Gateway, Bridge Cards.
Interface-readyFireblocks, Circle Programmable Wallets, Lit Protocol, Rain, Bridge Offramp.
Rejected / retiredPersona (too expensive), iDenfy (onboarding friction), Lithic production (needs $5M program commit).
Source of Truth

Go read the code, not this table, before quoting.

QuestionFile
Complete list of external configs?packages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/config.py (grep '^class.*Config')
Chain RPC URLs + chain IDs?packages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/config.py → ChainConfig list
Token contracts per chain?packages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/tokens.py
Card routing?CardStackConfig in config.py
Funding routing?FundingRoutingConfig in config.py
Deployed env vars?Cloud Run (API) + Vercel (dashboard + landing + checkout) project settings — source of truth.
